steps.title: Compare dots to the line
steps.content: The predicted $y$-value is the height of the line of best fit at the same $x$-value as a data point. A point has an actual $y$-value greater than predicted when its dot is above the line.

steps.title: Count the points above
steps.content: Counting the dots above the line of best fit gives $6$ data points.

steps.title: Match the count
steps.content: There are $6$ data points whose actual $y$-value is greater than the predicted $y$-value. The answer is C.
